citeinfo.origin: Department of Commerce (DOC), National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), National Ocean Service (NOS), Office for Coastal Management (OCM)
citeinfo.title: National Park Service Regions as of September 2003

pubinfo.publish: NOAA's Ocean Service, Office for Coastal Management (OCM)

descript.abstract: This layer represents regions of the National Park Service (NPS). NPS is a bureau within the United States Department of the Interior. The organization consists of a headquarters office, seven regional offices and multiple park and support units.
descript.purpose: To support coastal and ocean planning and other activities pursuant to the Energy Policy Act, Coastal Zone Management Act, Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act, National Environmental Policy Act, Rivers and Harbors Act and the Submerged Lands Act.

idinfo.useconst: These data are intended for coastal and ocean use planning. Not for navigation. http://www.marinecadastre.gov/about/disclaimer.html

attracc.attraccr: The attributes in this dataset are believed to be accurate.

dataqual.logic: These data are believed to be logically consistent. Geometry is topologically clean.
dataqual.complete: Spatial and attribute properties are believed to be complete, although attribute information has been simplified. Geometric thresholds from original data are preserved. No tests have been completed for exhaustiveness.

horizpa.horizpar: Maximum scale of intended use is 1:80,000.

citeinfo.origin: National Park Service, Intermountain GIS Program Office, Denver
citeinfo.title: Regions of the National Park Service, USA

pubinfo.publish: National Park Service, Intermountain GIS Program Office, Denver

procstep.procdesc: 1. Download NPS Regions dataset from https://irma.nps.gov/App/Reference/Profile/1035889 2. Dissolve polygons based on NPS_Region field 3. Edit the 'NPS_Region' field name so it matches the data dictionary 4. Create 'mailingAdress' and 'telephoneNumber' fields and populate with information from http://www.nps.gov/aboutus/contactinformation.htm 5. Define all field aliases 6. create/update metadata 7. Determine symbology for layer file. Create a layer file containing symbology from each feature class. Include Layer Name, Description, Credits, and Scale Ranges (if applicable) within the layer file and its source. Save in same location data lives in.

enttyp.enttypd: Regions of the National Park Service

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Length of feature in internal units.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Area of feature in internal units squared.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

distinfo.distliab: NOAA makes no warranties or representations whatsoever regarding the availability, quality, accuracy, content, completeness or suitability for the user's needs of such information. The services, information, and data made available on the MarineCadastre.gov web site are provided 'as is' without warranties of any kind. These data are intended for coastal and ocean use planning. Not for navigation. http://www.marinecadastre.gov/about/disclaimer.html

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
